focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm begins listening to a radio interview with Simon Foster, the Minister for International Development, discussing his progress after eighteen months in office.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm begins monitoring Simon Foster's radio interview, marking the start of his media surveillance duties in the scene.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: assess media performance of ministers
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: aware of Simon Foster's interview and actively listening to it
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: potential fallout from Simon's statements
evidence_quotes: A CIVIL SERVANT hands Malcolm a CD.
CIVIL SERVANT Monitoring. All the usual.
Malcolm starts listening to the recording of Simon on the radio.

relation_updates: established working rapport with Sam
status_updates: in office
engaged in media monitoring
auxiliary.persona_anchor: control-oriented civil servant enforcing political messaging

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A radio discussion on war and preventable diseases sparks frustration, prompting Malcolm to confront Simon over his remarks on international development.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm becomes visibly angered by Simon's radio comments on diarrhoea and war, and declares his intent to confront him physically.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: confront Simon Foster over inappropriate public remarks
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: decides to go to International Development to confront Simon Foster in person
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the confrontation with Simon will occur and how it will unfold
evidence_quotes: MALCOLM Oh yes, say it again. Very good. What is this, The Shitting Forecast?
MALCOLM is paying extremely close attention now.
MALCOLM NO YOU DO NOT THINK THAT! Sam! I'm going to have to go over to International Development, and pull Simon Foster's fucking hair.

relation_updates: escalated hostility toward Simon
status_updates: transitioning from observer to aggressor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: reactive authority figure asserting control through confrontation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm disputes someone's claim about a word being said, insisting it was not 'unforseeable' and emphasizes it as a fact.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm denies that the word 'unforseeable' was spoken, insisting it was not said despite others possibly hearing it.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to correct the record and assert authority over public statements
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: positions himself as the definitive source on what was officially said
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: discrepancy between what was heard and what is acknowledged as having been said
evidence_quotes: He did not say "unforseeable'.
You may have heard him say it, but he did not say that, and that is a fact.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Simon and Judy prepare a joke question when Malcolm unexpectedly arrives with Toby, interrupting them despite prior warning.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm enters Simon's office unannounced with Toby, dismisses Judy, and mocks Simon with a distorted quote about being 'unforseeable'.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to confront and intimidate Simon into compliance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Simon is caught off guard; Malcolm takes control of the space and initiates psychological pressure.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how Simon will respond to Malcolm's sudden intrusion and mockery
evidence_quotes: Malcolm walks in with Toby sheepishly behind him.
MALCOLM Now. And don't say you weren't prepared because I rang ahead.
(To JUDY) Give us a minute, will you love?

relation_updates: asserts dominance over Simon
uses Toby as a pawn in the confrontation
status_updates: establishes physical presence in Simon's office
reinforces narrative control by weaponizing the 'unforseeable' quote
auxiliary.persona_anchor: performative enforcer using public humiliation as a tool

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm exits, angrily instructing Simon and Judy to refine the media line, mocks Judy as 'Jane Austen with the strap-on,' suggests threatening the BBC, and tells Toby to handle it, deriding Judy and dismissing her concerns.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: senior political advisor controlling media messaging
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm halts Simon's media appearance, enforces strict message discipline, and publicly humiliates Judy to assert control over communications.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Prevent any unauthorized communication that could jeopardize U.S. relations
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Simon is barred from Question Time and restricted from speaking publicly; Judy is dismissed and mocked but remains defiant; Malcolm's control over messaging is enforced through intimidation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Judy's refusal to comply and Simon's continued dissent suggest ongoing resistance to Malcolm's authoritarian approach
evidence_quotes: That is not our line, alright? Walk the fucking line.
No, You're not going on Question Time tonight. You've been disinvited.
Hey, foetus boy. Lesson One: If I tell you to fuck off what do you do?

relation_updates: escalates conflict with Judy
asserts dominance over Simon
status_updates: consolidates power over media strategy
positions himself as indispensable crisis manager

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm, watching Sky News about Simon's press event, calls him annoyed about learning news publicly and demands he come over immediately.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm confronts Simon by phone after learning of his unapproved public statements through news coverage, demanding immediate accountability.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reassert authority over Simon and prevent unapproved messaging
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Malcolm reestablishes his position as the enforcer of internal discipline by directly challenging Simon’s actions.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Simon's public statements create ongoing risk of messaging leaks
evidence_quotes: He's watching the SKY NEWS coverage of Simon's mini-press coverage.

relation_updates: relationship with Simon becomes openly adversarial
status_updates: position as enforcer of party discipline is reaffirmed

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Simon is surprised by Malcolm's unexpected physical arrival, leading to a tense, sarcastic exchange between the two.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm arrives in person at Simon's location in Washington, shifting from remote contact to direct physical presence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain direct oversight of Simon in high-risk environment
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: is now physically present with Simon, increasing immediacy of control
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Simon feels unnerved by Malcolm's unexpected and pervasive presence
evidence_quotes: Simon has just answered the door to MALCOLM.
I'm here, I'm there, I'm fucking everywhere. I am the egg-man.
Come in....I just wasn't expecting you to be here. Physically here. Obviously, you're always in my heart.
Have you come to insult me in a different time zone?

relation_updates: dynamic with Simon shifts to constant surveillance
status_updates: establishes operational presence in international setting
auxiliary.persona_anchor: omnipresent operator extending control across borders

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm pressures Simon to stay neutral in an upcoming statement, rejecting conflicting narratives, and insists on rehearsing despite Simon's fatigue.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm insists Simon rehearse a neutral statement despite his fatigue, blocking attempts to disengage and enforcing message discipline.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ensure Simon delivers a controlled, non-contradictory statement under pressure.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Simon remains in rehearsal against his will, visibly exhausted and resistant.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Simon's fatigue and sense of coercion may compromise his performance or compliance.
evidence_quotes: SIMON Right. Can I go to bed now?
MALCOLM No, we're going to run that through.
SIMON Am I being tortured?

relation_updates: asserts dominance over Simon
status_updates: reinforces role as enforcer of political discipline
auxiliary.persona_anchor: relentless taskmaster indifferent to personal limits

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm criticizes Linton's team, feeling sidelined as Linton dismisses concerns with vague remarks about Munich, leaving Malcolm frustrated and excluded.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm confronts Linton about being excluded and receiving poor intelligence, but is brushed off with vague remarks, reinforcing his diminished role.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: asserting authority and demanding proper intelligence
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: angry and excluded
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: unclear meaning of 'All roads lead to Munich' and his lack of access to real intelligence
evidence_quotes: Are you fucking me about?
By the way, your prime minister informs me that he's tasked you with collating some fresh British intel for us.
What the fuck does that mean? `All roads lead to Munich'?
Come on let's go, get back to the hotel, nick as many coat hangers as you can. We're off back to London.

relation_updates: hostile interaction with Linton
status_updates: formally assigned a task but treated as an outsider
emotionally alienated from the U.S. team
auxiliary.persona_anchor: frustrated operator losing access to power

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm assigns Jamie two tasks: eliminate the Pwip Pip leaker and delay Pwip Pip's release by spreading fear to prevent BBC action before the vote.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm instructs Jamie to locate and kill the Pwip Pip leaker and to prevent the BBC from releasing the story by spreading fear and doubt ahead of the UN vote.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ensure the Pwip Pip leak is contained and the BBC delays publication until after the UN vote.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Malcolm initiates a two-pronged covert response to suppress the leak and influence media behavior.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The identity and location of the leaker are unknown; the success of the operation depends on Jamie's actions.
evidence_quotes: Okay Jamie, two jobs. One: find the Pwip Pip leaker and kill them. That's one job. Job one has two parts.
Job two: stop Pwip Pip coming out. Sow enough seeds of fear and doubt that the fat arses at the BBC dither till after the vote, okay?

relation_updates: reinforces trust and emotional closeness with Jamie through directive intimacy ('I love you')
status_updates: asserts control over covert operations
initiates active damage control strategy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: determined operator using direct, high-stakes directives to control political fallout

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm's authority crumbles as Simon resigns post-vote, dismissing his pleas, while Malcolm threatens Toby into silence, revealing his desperation and isolation.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm fails to deliver intel or delay the vote, is publicly demeaned by Linton, and responds by threatening Toby after Simon announces his resignation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Maintain control and prevent further leaks despite losing credibility and command.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: operationally defeated, emotionally exposed, and increasingly unstable
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: No viable path to recover intel or delay the vote; his power base has disintegrated
evidence_quotes: Honestly? I haven't got it. We need more time.
You, sir, are a useless piece of `S' star star `T'.
Okay, well, right, after the vote, I resign.
If you repeat this to anyone I will pull your leg off, break it in two and stab you to death with your broken shin bone.

relation_updates: Simon Foster severs loyalty through resignation
Toby Wright shifts from ally to intimidated subordinate
status_updates: loses political leverage to Karen Linton
publicly humiliated as 'useless piece of S**t'
auxiliary.persona_anchor: defeated enforcer resorting to last-ditch threats

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm informs Simon he is fired over the wall incident, fabricates having spoken to the PM, threatens media retaliation if challenged, and demands a resignation letter, while Toby's attempt to praise General Miller is rebuffed.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm confronts Simon, declares him fired over the wall incident, fabricates having spoken to the Prime Minister, threatens media retaliation, and demands a resignation letter.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: enforce Simon's removal and assert control through intimidation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Simon is effectively dismissed; Malcolm consolidates power through deception and threats
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: legitimacy of Malcolm's authority and potential consequences of his false claims
evidence_quotes: It's not ridiculous. You're fired.
I have spoken to the Prime Minister. Whether it has happened or not is irrelevant. It is true.
I will marshall all the forces of media darkness to hound you to an assisted suicide.

relation_updates: escalates conflict with Linton to open hostility and personal insult
status_updates: completes retrieval and delivery of critical intelligence
publicly undermines Linton's authority
auxiliary.persona_anchor: defiant operator using deception and intimidation to enforce outcomes

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Malcolm begins as a behind-the-scenes enforcer monitoring ministerial media performance, quickly escalating into direct confrontation with Simon Foster over messaging control. He asserts dominance through intimidation, linguistic precision, and narrative manipulation, extending his reach to Washington to maintain oversight. As political tides shift, his authority erodes, culminating in desperate coercion and isolation after Simon's resignation. Despite tactical victories, Malcolm's power collapses under institutional resistance and ethical overreach.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm exits, angrily instructing Simon and Judy to refine the media line, mocks Judy as 'Jane Austen with the strap-on,' suggests threatening the BBC, and tells Toby to handle it, deriding Judy and dismissing her concerns.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm bans Simon from Question Time and all media appearances for deviating from the official line, rejecting his attempt to justify his comments as a personal opinion.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 17、INT. FOREIGN OFFICE GRAND MEETING ROOM - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Simon retracts his earlier stance as mutable, but Michael redirects the meeting; Suzy messages Judy to stop Simon's behavior, Judy complies, and Simon, unsettled by Toby, remains silent amid ongoing tension.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Simon attempts to clarify his position on the foreseeability of war but is physically restrained by Toby, interrupted by Michael redirecting the meeting, and receives a note from Suzy via Judy calling him disruptive; he remains silent after being whispered to by Toby.

timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how and when he will respond to being publicly undermined and privately shamed

But that doesn't mean that what I said won't ever change. It's not imutable. Or mutable. It's an ongoing ...
He's ended the debate.

diminished standing with Suzy and Michael, who see him as disruptive

position exposed as marginal within the power structure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm pressures Simon about a Washington fact-finding trip amid Middle East concerns, mocks his indecision, warns him to avoid Linton Barwick, and advises minimal contact while there.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: cabinet minister under pressure from senior adviser

timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Simon will take any concrete position on the war

timeline_nodes.salient_development: Karen and General Miller plan to manipulate Simon Foster to broaden dissent within a military committee by leveraging his public stance on war.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Simon Foster's arc traces a descent from anxious minister under scrutiny to a politically isolated figure ultimately forced out of office. Initially preoccupied with image and media performance, he gradually confronts moral conflict over war, briefly asserting independence before being manipulated and silenced. His attempts to regain agency are undermined by powerful figures, culminating in a coerced dismissal framed around a trivial scandal. The trajectory reveals a man overwhelmed by systemic pressure, whose personal and political disintegration is accelerated by betrayal and performative control.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 14、INT. DFID - DAY / INT. SIMON'S OFFICE - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Toby secures Simon a spot in the Karen Clark meeting at the Foreign Office, impressing Judy, who recalls his earlier 'meat' comment, while Simon downplays Malcolm's abrasive behavior.

Toby gains minor status elevation through demonstrated initiative

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 17、INT. FOREIGN OFFICE GRAND MEETING ROOM - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Simon retracts his earlier stance as mutable, but Michael redirects the meeting; Suzy messages Judy to stop Simon's behavior, Judy complies, and Simon, unsettled by Toby, remains silent amid ongoing tension.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: intervener in Simon's moment of vulnerability
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Toby uses physical and verbal cues to prevent Simon from speaking further during the meeting, helping to suppress his outburst.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to maintain decorum and prevent Simon from disrupting the meeting
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Simon stops speaking and remains silent, visibly displeased after Toby's intervention
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Simon may seek to reclaim agency after being restrained
evidence_quotes: Toby maybe puts a calming hand on his hand.
Toby whispers something in Simon's ear.
Simon shuts up. He'll have this out later.

relation_updates: asserts authority over Simon
aligns with Judy in managing Simon
status_updates: emerges as a moderating force in team dynamics

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Simon plans to take Toby to the US, excluding Judy; Toby agrees, criticizing Judy, and Simon decides she's staying behind.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: chosen team member in Simon's inner circle
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Toby is selected by Simon for the US trip and participates in mocking Judy, contributing to her exclusion from the delegation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure position on US delegation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: formally included in 'Team Simon' while Judy is left behind
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension likely to arise from aligning with Simon against a senior colleague
evidence_quotes: So listen. My team for the US. Team Simon. I'm thinking of taking you and leaving Judy?
I could work with that, definitely. Plus she can be a bit... you know? "Everything's a bit shit isn't it?"
Okay. It's settled. Fuck it. She's staying behind. Go and tell her.

relation_updates: elevated status relative to Judy
complicit in mocking Judy's attitude
status_updates: officially part of Team Simon

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Toby struggles to answer a call from Matty with gel-covered hands, muffling the conversation while explaining his upcoming role on Liza's committee, which he calls the 'war committee.'
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Toby confirms his assignment to Liza Weld's Future Planning Committee, which he informally refers to as the 'war committee,' indicating his anticipation of high-stakes political work.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to prepare for and succeed in the war committee
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Toby is officially assigned to the Future Planning Committee and begins preparing for his role
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he will meet expectations in this new role
evidence_quotes: Liza Weld's's got us on to Linton's Future Planning Committee in the morning.
The war committee, to you and me.
Yes, Future Planning Committee is the war committee.
Very excited. Starts getting ready to see Liza.

relation_updates: developing professional connection with Liza Weld
status_updates: assigned to the Future Planning Committee

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Toby and Liza are kissing on the bed; Toby jokes about keeping things secret with a Washington phrase, which Liza points out doesn't work since she lives there.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Toby attempts to invoke a rule of secrecy about the encounter using a modified 'what happens in Washington' line, showing an awareness of the need for discretion in political contexts.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain discretion around personal relationships
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: engaged in a physically intimate moment with Liza, a key political contact, without established agreement on confidentiality
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: no mutual understanding of confidentiality has been reached, as Liza points out the impracticality of Toby's proposed secrecy
evidence_quotes: Toby and Liza are on the bed together, kissing.
TOBY Could I just say, you know, that what happens in Washington stays in Washington?
LIZA Yeah I live in Washington. So that doesn't really work for me.

relation_updates: engages in intimate moment with Liza
status_updates: enters into a private, potentially compromising situation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Liza questions Toby's relationship with Linton sarcastically, reflecting tension and discomfort amid ongoing committee pressures and personal conflicts.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: subordinate facing reprimand and navigating strained personal dynamics
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Toby is publicly criticized by Simon for arriving late and unprepared to a major meeting, and exchanges awkward pleasantries with Liza, highlighting ongoing tension.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to deflect blame and reduce fallout from his late arrival
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: defensive and visibly uncomfortable after being mocked by his superior and encountering Liza
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his reliability is questioned by Simon, and his relationship with Liza remains tense and unaddressed
evidence_quotes: So I turned up late to the meeting Simon. I'm sorry. But it's not like I threw up in there.
Toby mouths `shit' to himself.

relation_updates: conflict with Simon escalates
unresolved history with Liza resurfaces
status_updates: professionally chastised
emotionally exposed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: under pressure from professional missteps and personal friction

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Toby attempts to leave a memory stick containing Liza's paper that could stop a war, but after Suzy mocks him and Michael interrupts, he leaves it behind and departs amid hostility.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Toby attempts to leave a memory stick with evidence that could stop the war but backs down when mocked, abandoning the act under social pressure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to expose Liza's paper to prevent war
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: abandons attempt to share incriminating evidence after being ridiculed by Suzy and Michael
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether to act on politically sensitive information
evidence_quotes: TOBY Look, Suzy, this is probably going to sound odd under the circumstances.
TOBY Listen, Suze, Liza wrote a paper, Pwip- Pip. I think, if it got leaked, it could stop the war. He holds out the memory stick.
MICHAEL Good tactic. Get earnest. I tried that with the wife. Didn't work.
SUZY You are such a fucking coward, you know that?

relation_updates: strained relationship with Suzy intensifies
Michael aligns with Suzy against Toby

rejected from shared home

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Malcolm places Toby on indefinite probation, asserts control over him, and threatens Ambassador Tutt to delay a vote, using intimidation to enforce compliance.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Malcolm accuses Toby of leaking classified information and places him on indefinite probation, asserting dominance through threats.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: survival under pressure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: intimidated and compliant
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: fear of exposure and retaliation
evidence_quotes: I know it was you who leaked Linton's War Committee.
You're my guy now. I own you now. You're my Kunte Kinte.

relation_updates: Malcolm asserts dominance over Toby
Toby becomes Malcolm's subordinate under threat

under threat of severe retaliation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Toby collects his belongings and leaves as the new Minister and her advisor, Danny, arrive and settle in, taking over his desk while Malcolm and Judy greet them.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Toby is replaced as chief advisor, with his desk immediately reassigned to the incoming advisor Danny, and he departs the office without interaction or acknowledgment.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: former advisor, no longer part of the team
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: what comes next after being discarded
evidence_quotes: In the background we see Toby getting the last of his things together.
Toby grabs the last of his things, glances over at them, and them we follow him as he heads down the front steps.
She points at Toby's desk.
Hi I'm Danny. Dan. I'm Elizabeth's chief advisor.

relation_updates: displaced by new advisor Danny
status_updates: removed from position
desk reassigned

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Toby Wright begins as an idealistic newcomer to the high-pressure world of political advising, eager to prove himself but initially dismissed and mocked. Over time, he gains minor influence through initiative and alignment with superiors, only to be drawn into morally compromising situations and subjected to systemic abuse of power. His personal and professional boundaries erode under pressure, culminating in complicity, isolation, and eventual dismissal. The arc traces his transformation from hopeful outsider to silenced subordinate, ultimately discarded without resolution.
